# Workspace / Native Config SSOT: Native Config Manifest

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/workspacenativeconfigdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to workspace](../workspace.md)

Executable SSOT: [`native-config-manifest.riido.json`](native-config-manifest.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `workspace-native-config-docs`.

This SSOT fixes the C6 native config manifest, deterministic materialization, NativeConfigVersion, workspace lock policy, adjacent contracts, and closed workdir decisions as generated documentation.

The executable file-plan source remains `internal/workdir/native_config_plan.riido.json`; this manifest generates the reader-facing boundary and verifies source anchors around the generated plan and workdir implementation.

## Executable Assertions

- The provider native config file plan is generated from `internal/workdir/native_config_plan.riido.json`; code must not redefine provider filename or hook mapping ad hoc.
- `riido-native-config-manifest.v1` is replayable evidence written into the workdir and native-config copy.
- `NativeConfigVersion` is deterministic, policy-bound, and content-bound for all injected files.
- Native config overlay is per-task by default; user-global config copy/overlay requires explicit C7 surface and C6 manifest/version changes.
- C6 owns host-side workdir and manifest materialization, while C4 owns runtime launch and C7 owns security policy decisions.
- Q-WS decisions stay closed as executable C6/C7/runtime evidence and must not drift back into roadmap prose.
